message:
runtime: tighten cast failure semantics and cancellation cleanup invariants

Context:
This commit hardens runtime behavior around bounded mailbox pressure,
I/O registration lifecycle, and cancellation/obligation protocol checks.

What changed:
- `src/gen_server.rs`
  - `DropOldest` cast paths now treat `SendError::Full` as a recoverable
    `CastError::Full` instead of assuming it is unreachable.
  - `SendError::Cancelled` is classified with disconnected/stopped behavior,
    returning `CastError::ServerStopped`.
  - Added regression tests for both `GenServerHandle::try_cast` and
    `GenServerRef::try_cast` proving that a reserved mailbox slot in a
    capacity-1 server produces `CastError::Full` (not panic/unreachable).

- `src/runtime/io_driver.rs`
  - Reworked `IoRegistration::deregister` to stop using `mem::forget(self)`.
  - Added explicit `deregistered: bool` state so successful explicit
    deregistration suppresses duplicate best-effort deregistration in `Drop`.
  - Preserves retry-once behavior for transient lock races while ensuring
    idempotent cleanup semantics.

- `tests/cancel_obligation_invariants.rs`
  - Added `repeated_cancel_request_is_idempotent_during_drain` to verify
    repeated cancel requests do not violate protocol progression and do not
    leak obligations when drain/finalize completes.
  - Added `repeated_cancel_request_does_not_mask_partial_drain_leak` to prove
    repeated cancel requests cannot hide unresolved obligation leaks.

Why this matters:
- Bounded mailbox saturation now reports correct, actionable backpressure
  instead of relying on an unreachable assumption.
- Explicit deregistration no longer relies on object leak patterns and avoids
  duplicate deregistration side effects.
- Cancellation invariants are enforced for repeated-request scenarios,
  preserving protocol correctness and leak detection guarantees.
